title: Explain atomic classes in Java. When should you use them?

answers.junior: Atomic classes provide thread-safe operations for variables like int and boolean.
answers.middle: Atomic classes (e.g., AtomicInteger) allow lock-free, thread-safe updates. Use them for counters and flags.
answers.senior: Atomic classes use CAS (compare-and-swap) for efficient concurrency. Prefer them for simple shared state. For complex logic, use locks or concurrent collections.
answers.tricky: Use atomic classes for lock-free concurrency. Analyze performance and contention. Prefer atomic operations for scalability. Combine with other concurrency tools for robust design.
